title: Remote; difference between msi and zip on Windows
body: To provide the podman support with CRC we would like to use an easy way to embed/provide the client binary to install on the user's machine. using the `*.msi` is not preferred, but we noticed the `*.zip` is not made the same. See: https://github.com/code-ready/crc/issues/961#issuecomment-594913942

comments.body:
https://github.com/containers/libpod/releases/tag/v1.8.1  <-- how does this work!

fwiw, i put the msi there and a zip containing just the podman-windows-remote.exe and a docs dir with the html files
Looks good, do we have the readme.md links to there?
Should we have MAC Version there as well.

Why are the contens of the Windows zip archive so different from the MSI and also when compared to even the macOS release? `podman-remote-windows` and `podman`

`podman-remote-latest-master-darwin-amd64.zip - ZIP archive, unpacked size 50,308,978 bytes`
```
podman-v1.6.2-75-g6c6e7837/docs/
podman-v1.6.2-75-g6c6e7837/podman
```

`podman-remote-windows.zip - ZIP archive, unpacked size 51,827,111 bytes`
```
docs/
podman-remote-windows.exe
```

Note the names and the additional directory. (It is compared with different versions, but this does not affect the assessment as it was always like this :-( )
look, i did what i thought would be helpful.  given this is almost entirely being done for you, what do you want?  i could just zip the executable and drop the docs.  let me know.

>  you

`/s/you/CRC`. I think #minikube and @afbjorklund could also benefit from this.

The process for extracting and handling these archives should not have to inlvolve additional platform logic to deal with. It is NOT about having docs in there, as I could care less about those, but the subdirectory and the differently named platform executable (`podman`, `podman-remote-windows.exe`).

Are these created by a build script? If so, let me know since if this seems too much I can patch it.
Yes I agree, the executable should be podman.exe not podman-remote-windows.exe

That looks better. Can this be available on the same location for all
platforms?
From the code we use google storage:
https://storage.googleapis.com/libpod-master-releases/

It would be great to have a consistent location with tags
